parameterGroups.label: CC-to-Note Mapping
parameterGroups.rationale: Both parameters control the CC-to-note conversion: Bypass gates the entire process, ccSelector chooses which CC number triggers notes

gaps.question: How does CC2Note transform incoming CC messages into note events in processHiseEvent()? Does it generate noteOn when CC value > 0 and noteOff when CC value == 0, or is there a different threshold/mapping?
gaps.context: Users need to understand the exact CC-value-to-note conversion to use this module correctly.

gaps.question: How is the generated note number determined? Is it derived from the CC value, a fixed note, or some other mapping? Similarly, how is the velocity of the generated note determined?
gaps.context: Users need to know what note number and velocity the generated events will have to set up their sound generators correctly.

gaps.question: What happens to CC messages that do not match the selected CC number? Are they passed through unchanged, or are all CC messages consumed/filtered?
gaps.context: Users need to know whether placing this module in the MIDI chain affects other CC messages.

gaps.question: Are existing noteOn/noteOff events in the MIDI stream passed through, or does CC2Note only output generated notes?
gaps.context: Determines whether this module can coexist with keyboard input or replaces it entirely.

gaps.question: When Bypass is toggled on while a note is active (CC value was nonzero), does the module send a noteOff for the active note or leave it hanging?
gaps.context: Active note cleanup during bypass is important for avoiding stuck notes.

gaps.question: The base data description mentions 'round-robin triggering'. Is there actual round-robin logic in the module, or is this referring to a use case where the user sets up round-robin downstream?
gaps.context: The description should accurately reflect the module's own capabilities versus suggested workflows.

notes: CC2Note is a simple MidiProcessor with only two parameters and no modulation chains or interfaces. The description mentions 'round-robin triggering' which may be a use-case suggestion rather than built-in functionality - this needs C++ verification. The ccSelector default of 0 means it listens to CC#0 (Bank Select MSB) by default, which is an unusual default worth noting.
